programs.name: Community Choices Waiver
programs.what_it_does: The Community Choices Waiver enables Louisiana seniors requiring nursing home level care to live safely in their own home or assisted living community. It covers services like case management, personal assistance, adult day health care, therapy, meal delivery, and nursing.[2]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 55+ certified as needing nursing home level care, financially eligible for Medicaid Long-Term Care Services, and living in waiver service areas.[2]
programs.whats_unique: This is a Louisiana-specific Medicaid Home and Community-Based Services (HCBS) waiver program tailored to state needs, not available nationwide as a standard federal program.[2]
programs.benefit_value: Covers some or all costs of approved long-term care services to prevent nursing home placement.[2]

programs.name: Louisiana Long Term–Personal Care Services (LT-PCS) Program
programs.what_it_does: LT-PCS provides in-home assistance with Activities of Daily Living (ADLs) like bathing, dressing, and medication reminders, and Instrumental ADLs (IADLs) like meal prep, laundry, and housecleaning. It aims to prevent or delay nursing home admissions or support transitions home from facilities.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Louisiana residents aged 65+ (elderly) or 21+ (disabled) who reside in a nursing home and can relocate with aid, are anticipated to need nursing home care within 120 days, or have a primary caregiver aged 70+ or disabled; must self-direct care.[3]
programs.whats_unique: Offered as a state plan Medicaid benefit through Healthy Louisiana managed care, it's Louisiana-specific for preventing institutionalization without support coordination.[3]
programs.benefit_value: In-home personal care services to maintain independence; can combine with waivers like Adult Day Health Care Waiver.[3]

programs.name: Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE) - Louisiana
programs.what_it_does: Louisiana PACE delivers comprehensive health, primary, long-term, and acute care to help seniors aged 55+ live healthily in the community. It covers all Medicaid and Medicare services, including nutritional counseling, prescription drugs, and adult day care.[2]
programs.who_its_for: Aged 55+, certified needing nursing home level care, Medicaid Long-Term Care eligible, living in PACE areas: Baton Rouge, Greater New Orleans, or Lafayette.[2]
programs.whats_unique: State-implemented with specific provider service areas in Louisiana; while PACE exists federally, local rollout and eligibility are Louisiana-tailored.[2]
programs.benefit_value: Full coverage of Medicare/Medicaid services including prescriptions and day care for community living.[2]
